element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Players learn basic economy through harvesting and refining Tiberium deposits
MCV deployment teaches spatial planning and structure adjacency rules
Basic combat against weak enemy forces
Introduces combined arms tactics and unit counters
Players learn perimeter defense and resource allocation trade-offs
Technology progression unlocks superior unit types
GDI Ion Cannon, Nod Nuclear Missile as mission objectives or threats
Players must manage multiple threat vectors simultaneously

typical_tension_triggers: Allied base destroyed in co-op scenarios
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Wave-based escalation with resource attrition. Early calm allows economy setup; mid-game features escalating enemy waves and map control struggles; late-game forces decisive confrontation or bases reach defensive saturation. Tension rises with visible enemy production and depleting resources.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Tiberium field density and positioning; side areas with bonus ore patches reward exploration and early harvester routes
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Hidden Tiberium fields in map corners; bonus infantry/vehicles scattered in unreachable-seeming terrain (reward for unit persistence)

difficulty_curve.early_game: Minimal opposition, focus on learning base construction and resource flow. First few missions feature weak or non-aggressive enemies.
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Enemy bases activate production. Player manages multiple threats: expanding enemy territory, Tiberium competition, defensive pressure. Introduced to faction-specific units and counters.
difficulty_curve.late_game: Aggressive multi-pronged attacks; superweapon threats; time-pressured objectives. Player base fully developed but stretched thin managing multiple fronts.
notable_spikes: First timed objective requiring aggressive early expansion
Introduction of enemy superweapon charge (Ion Cannon/Nuclear Missile)
Final campaign missions forcing invasion of heavily fortified enemy bases
GDI vs Nod campaign divergence introducing faction-specific challenges

notable_level_patterns.description: Mission centers on securing and holding multiple Tiberium fields. Player must defend harvesters and processing plants while denying enemy access. Spatial layout emphasizes field positioning and harvester route efficiency.
Fixed player base under waves of escalating enemy attacks. Defensive structures become primary investment. Success requires wall placement, turret positioning, and unit reserves for counterattacks.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Campaign missions with 'defend until reinforcements arrive' objectives

notable_level_patterns.description: Player attacks pre-built enemy base with defined construction yard and defenses. Requires reconnaissance, unit composition planning, and coordinated multi-vector assault to overwhelm static defenses.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Late-campaign objectives; skirmish maps against AI

notable_level_patterns.description: Protect mobile units or structures moving across map toward objective. Vulnerability window creates tension; requires escort units and path management.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Campaign missions transporting MCVs or VIPs to new positions

notable_level_patterns.description: Race to build/charge faction superweapon before enemy detonates theirs. Creates time pressure and forces aggressive economy or sabotage tactics.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Late GDI/Nod missions featuring Ion Cannon or Nuclear Missile
